common.errorBody: SeaCheck hit an unexpected problem. Tap Retry to continue. If this keeps happening, restart the app.

ui.sheetBackdropHint: Tap outside to close this panel

onboarding.title: Welcome to SeaCheck
onboarding.disclaimerBody: SeaCheck is not a certified chart plotter. Always carry official charts and verify critical navigational information. Map and seamark data may be incomplete or outdated.
onboarding.locationBody: SeaCheck needs your GPS position to show you on the chart and display COG, SOG, and bearing.
onboarding.locationForeground: Allow location while using the app
onboarding.locationOrderHint: Allow foreground location first, then background.
onboarding.locationReady: Location access is set up. Continuing…
onboarding.batteryBody: On Android, battery savers often stop GPS updates at sea. Disable battery optimization for SeaCheck and allow background location for anchor alarms and track recording.
onboarding.downloadReminder: On Wi‑Fi, open Downloads and save the Kieler Bucht pack — charts work offline at sea.
onboarding.notificationsBody: SeaCheck needs notifications for anchor drag and arrival alarms when the screen is off.

boot.failedBody: Something went wrong while loading your data. Check storage space and try again.
boot.partialWarning: Some data could not be loaded. Charts and alarms may be limited until you restart the app.
boot.partialWarningDismissHint: Hides this startup notice for this session
boot.partialWarningOffline: Offline charts could not be loaded. Download packs again while on Wi‑Fi.
boot.partialWarningNavigation: Navigation state could not be restored.
boot.partialWarningLocale: Language preference could not be loaded.
boot.partialWarningMultiple: Some data could not be loaded: %{items}. Restart the app after fixing storage or connectivity.

permissions.foregroundNeededBody: SeaCheck needs GPS to show your position and run alarms. Allow location, or continue with limited features.
permissions.backgroundNeededBody: Anchor alarms with the screen off require “Always allow” location in system settings.
permissions.backgroundGrantedHint: Background location enabled — anchor alarms and track recording work with screen off.
permissions.backgroundDeniedHint: Background location denied — anchor alarms only work while the app is open.
permissions.backgroundBlockedHint: Background location was blocked — open system settings and choose “Always allow”.
permissions.foregroundGrantedHint: Location enabled — GPS position and alarms are available.
permissions.foregroundDeniedHint: Location denied — SeaCheck needs GPS for navigation and alarms.
permissions.foregroundBlockedHint: Location was blocked — open system settings to enable GPS access.
permissions.reducedAccuracyHint: Precise location is off — enable full accuracy in system settings for reliable anchor alarms.

location.backgroundAnchorBody: Monitoring anchor position in the background
location.backgroundPassageBody: Monitoring position for arrival and cross-track alarms

settings.title: Settings
settings.homeSummary: Choose a category to adjust display, map, units, vessel, GPS, alarms, or about.

menu.displaySummary: Theme, layout, coordinates, and glove mode
menu.mapSummary: Map behaviour, chart data, and downloads
menu.vesselSummary: Boat name, call sign, MMSI, and home port
menu.gpsSummary: Location permissions, battery, and background tracking
menu.alarmsSummary: Notifications, sound, limits, and anchor watch
menu.aboutSummary: Disclaimer, emergency copy, and app version

settings.displaySummary: Theme, chart data, coordinates, and map behaviour
settings.coordFormatHint: Choose how latitude and longitude appear when you read or copy your position. Marine charts usually use degrees and decimal minutes.
settings.activityProfileHint: Sets default layout and units for your type of boating.
settings.layoutSummary: Choose how the map and instruments share the screen. Saved separately for phone, tablet, and orientation.

layoutDescriptions.map-forward: Map on top with instruments and your live coordinates below — everyday default for sailing and passage.
layoutDescriptions.instruments-only: Full-screen instruments with no chart — maximum number space and lower battery use. Switch to any other layout to show the map again.
layoutDescriptions.minimal: Full-screen chart with speed and course only — maximum map space; safety buttons on the right map edge.

settings.courseVectorHint: Shows a long heading line from the boat icon at your current speed so you can see where you are going.
settings.courseVectorMinutesHint: How many minutes ahead the line projects at your current speed — 6 minutes is the usual chart-plotter default (up to 20 for longer legs).
settings.courseVectorScaleHint: Makes the heading line longer or shorter for easier reading — like Navionics. Projection time stays the same.
settings.courseVectorZoomHint: When zoomed out, the line may draw longer than the true speed projection so it stays visible on screen. Navigation always uses your speed and projection time.
settings.followZoomHint: How close the chart zooms when follow mode centres on your GPS position.
settings.gpsSmoothPositionHint: Reduces position jitter on the map using accuracy-weighted filtering. Anchor alarm and MOB always use the raw GPS fix.
settings.barometerEnabledHint: Shows pressure and trend in the instrument panel. Needs a pressure sensor in your device — off by default.
settings.gloveModeHint: Increases buttons to 56 px for wet hands or gloves
settings.mapShowPassageRouteLinesHint: Connect waypoints with lines on the main chart while planning or following a passage.
settings.mapShowRecordingDistanceHint: Shows a chip with live track distance while recording. Tap it to open Tracks. Off by default — full stats stay on the Tracks tab.
settings.chartDataSummary: Base map and OpenSeaMap seamarks — what SeaCheck downloads and shows offline.
settings.chartDataLayersBody: SeaCheck uses Carto Voyager base tiles plus OpenSeaMap seamark tiles. Both are included in offline region packs.
settings.sogUnitHint: How your speed over ground is displayed (knots are standard at sea).
settings.bearingRefHint: True uses north on the chart. Magnetic matches a steering compass when variation is applied.
settings.defaultsSummary: Cruise/passage · knots · nautical miles · true north · degrees & decimal minutes · course-up
settings.batteryBody: Keep SeaCheck exempt from battery optimization on Android so GPS, anchor alarms, and track recording stay reliable underway.
settings.anchorBackgroundHint: Anchor alarms with screen off need “Always allow” location. SeaCheck starts background GPS automatically when an anchor alarm is set.
settings.batteryRestricted: Battery: optimization may stop GPS in background
settings.backgroundGpsStopped: Background GPS: not running — set an anchor alarm or enable background tracks
settings.alarmNotificationsOn: Notifications on — alarms can alert you when the screen is off.
settings.alarmNotificationsOff: Notifications off — enable for reliable anchor alerts in the background.
settings.alarmNotificationsDenied: Notifications denied — open system settings to enable.
settings.locationDenied: Location: denied — enable in system settings
settings.alarmsBody: Sound and vibration for anchor drag, arrival, and off-course alerts.
settings.alarmLimitsUnitNote: Distances use your chosen unit (%{unit}). Stored precisely for alarms.
settings.alarmArrivalHint: Circle around each waypoint that counts as “arrived”. SeaCheck also advances when you pass abeam while tacking or cutting a corner. %{example} %{unit} is a good default for coastal sailing.
settings.anchorWatchHint: Default drag radius when you set an anchor alarm from the chart.
settings.downloadsSummary: Same Wi‑Fi setting as the Downloads tab — large chart packs stay off cellular unless you confirm.
settings.legAdvanceAutoHint: When a waypoint is reached or passed, move to the next leg automatically. You can always skip manually on the map banner.
settings.disclaimerBody: SeaCheck is an aid to navigation only. Not for primary navigation. Always verify with official charts.

map.copyCoordsHint: Copies the current position to the clipboard
map.downloadHint: Download a region pack on Wi‑Fi before going offshore — charts work offline once saved.
map.offlineChartAlertDismissHint: Hides this offline chart warning until you are back online
map.downloadHintOpenHint: Opens Downloads to save charts for offline use
map.chartA11yBoatStaleAtCenter: Last known position at chart centre — GPS stale
map.chartA11yFollow: Follow mode active — chart tracks your position
map.chartA11yPan: Follow paused — you moved the chart
map.chartA11yHint: Pan with one finger to move the chart. Pinch to zoom. Press and hold the chart to open actions for that spot. Tap a waypoint or track point for details.
map.chartA11yOfflineNoCharts: Offline with no downloaded charts in this area
map.chartA11yOfflineUncovered: Offline — current view is outside downloaded chart coverage
map.chartInitFailedBody: SeaCheck could not prepare the map. Check free storage and try again.
map.offlineCacheOnlyHint: Recently viewed tiles may be missing or outdated. Not for navigation — download a pack on Wi‑Fi.
map.chartsNotHereHint: Recent cache may still show some tiles. Pan to a downloaded region or open Downloads.
map.gpsAccuracyGood: GPS accuracy plus or minus %{m} metres
map.gpsAccuracyWarn: GPS accuracy poor — plus or minus %{m} metres
map.gpsOutlierHint: A sudden GPS jump was ignored for the map. Anchor and MOB still use the raw signal.
map.gpsStale: GPS signal lost — last fix too old
map.recenterHint: Centres the map on your position and resumes follow mode
map.locationMenuTitle: What do you want to do here?
map.locationMenuHint: Add a waypoint, set anchor watch, copy coordinates, or look up a seamark.
map.seamarkLookupNone: No seamark found at this spot
map.seamarkLookupFailed: Could not look up seamark — try again when online
map.screenLockHint: Blocks accidental touches while sailing. MOB stays available below. Hold the centre to unlock.
map.screenLockMobHint: Man overboard: hold the red MOB button below for 2 seconds.
map.unlockScreenHint: Hold for one and a half seconds to unlock the screen
map.emergencyCopyStale: Mayday text copied with last known position — GPS is stale or inaccurate. Verify before transmitting.
map.emergencyNoGpsBody: No reliable position available. Wait for GPS before copying a Mayday message.
map.anchorWatchMonitoringHint: Monitors your GPS position continuously while the alarm is on. You are alerted if you drift outside the green circle or move steadily while at anchor.
map.anchorRadiusHint: Drag alarm radius — choose before setting the alarm, or change it here while the watch is active.
map.mobHoldHint: Hold for two seconds to drop a MOB mark at your current position
map.mobDroppedBody: Navigate back using bearing and distance. MOB appears on map and in waypoints.
map.mobNoGpsBody: Wait for a GPS fix before dropping a MOB mark.
map.mobNavigateBackHint: Steer to the bearing and distance shown. Tap Clear when the person is recovered.
map.mobClearBody: Stops navigate-back mode. The MOB waypoint stays saved in your list.
map.mobDropFailed: Could not save the MOB mark. Try again when GPS is stable.
map.coordFormatCycleHint: Long press to switch how your position is written
map.seamarkUnknownBody: No seamark metadata at this spot. Tap coordinates to copy.
map.safetyBarA11y: Safety actions: screen lock, anchor watch, man overboard
map.anchorBackgroundBody: Allow background location so SeaCheck can alert you if you drift while the screen is locked or another app is open.
map.anchorSetBody: Alert if you drift more than %{value} %{unit} from this position.
map.anchorSetLimitedBody: Anchor set at %{value} %{unit} radius — screen-off monitoring is limited until background location and notifications are enabled.
map.anchorSetFailed: Could not set anchor alarm. Try again.
map.anchorWatchLimitedBody: Without background location and alarm notifications, anchor alerts only work while SeaCheck is open on screen.
map.anchorAlreadyActive: Anchor alarm is already active — clear it first or choose a new position on the map.
map.anchorMapNoGpsBody: Your current position is unknown. The anchor alarm will be set at the point you selected on the chart.
map.anchorMapPositionBody: The selected point is far from your current GPS position. Confirm you want the anchor watch centred there.
map.anchorReplaceBody: Replaces the current anchor position and resets drag monitoring.
map.anchorNoGpsBody: Wait for a GPS fix to set the anchor alarm at your current position.
map.layoutSettingsA11y: Current layout %{layout}. Opens Settings to change layout.
map.layoutSettingsHint: Opens Settings where you can choose a map layout

coordinates.blockHint: Tap a row to copy that version of your latitude and longitude.
coordinates.copyHint: Copies this format to the clipboard

ddm.title: Degrees & decimal minutes
ddm.description: Standard on marine charts and in radio calls.

dd.title: Decimal degrees
dd.description: One number for latitude and one for longitude — common in apps and GPS exports.

dms.title: Degrees, minutes & seconds
dms.description: Traditional notation — sometimes used on older charts and in aviation.

alarms.anchorGpsSuspended: GPS signal lost — anchor drag detection paused until fix returns
alarms.anchorSogBody: Moving at %{kn} kn while at anchor
alarms.legAdvanceBody: You are at or past %{name}. Start the next leg?

downloads.title: Offline charts
downloads.subtitle: Download base map + OpenSeaMap seamarks for use without signal.
downloads.howChartsWorkLead: Two ways to see charts without signal — pick what fits your trip.
downloads.howChartsRecentBody: SeaCheck keeps about %{size} MB of tiles from recent online use. Unreliable for passage planning — download a pack for full coverage.
downloads.howChartsPackBody: Corridor or custom packs are saved on this device. Reliable charts and seamarks in that area.
downloads.wifiNote: Download corridor-sized packs on Wi‑Fi — each area shows estimated size before you start (like NV Charts). Kieler Bucht is a small test pack.
downloads.wifiOnlyHint: When on, cellular downloads require confirmation
downloads.cellularWarnBody: Large chart downloads use mobile data. Continue anyway?
downloads.cellularCancelledBody: Chart downloads on mobile data need your confirmation. Connect to Wi‑Fi or allow the download when prompted.
downloads.deleteConfirmBody: Remove “%{name}” and its cached charts from this device? You can download it again later on Wi‑Fi.
downloads.seamarksPending: Seamarks not indexed — connect to Wi‑Fi on Downloads
downloads.errorPackUnavailable: Could not verify offline pack — try again
downloads.errorDownloadBusy: Another chart download is already running. Wait for it to finish or cancel it first.
downloads.errorOffline: No internet connection — connect to Wi‑Fi or mobile data to download charts.
downloads.errorTileServerTimeout: Chart tile server did not respond in time. Check your connection and try again.
downloads.errorTileServerUnavailable: Chart tile server is temporarily unavailable. Try again in a few minutes.
downloads.errorTileServerUnreachable: Cannot reach chart tile servers. Check your connection or try another network.
downloads.errorSeamarkTileServerUnreachable: Cannot reach seamark tile servers. Check your connection or try again in a few minutes.
downloads.errorDownloadStalled: Download stopped making progress. Check your connection and try again.
downloads.errorMapEngineStyle: Chart engine did not start. Close and reopen the app, then try again on Wi‑Fi.
downloads.errorDownloadInterrupted: Download was interrupted — tap Try again to resume.
downloads.errorStorageInsufficient: Not enough free storage for this download. Free space and try again.
downloads.errorPackTooLarge: This chart pack exceeds the safe download limit — choose a smaller corridor pack or use a custom area.
downloads.errorPackRetired: This region pack was replaced by smaller corridor downloads and cannot be downloaded again.
downloads.downloadSuccess: Chart pack ready for offline use
downloads.downloadAlreadyActive: Download already in progress — see the banner at the top.
downloads.regionPacksP0Hint: Essential corridor packs for common passages in this region.
downloads.testPackHint: Small area for dockside testing around Kiel.
downloads.passageRecommendedBody: From your passage plan — download these to cover chart gaps.
downloads.passageCustomBody: Some legs of “%{name}” need a custom chart rectangle. Open the map to review and adjust the suggested bounds.
downloads.largePackHint: Large download — use Wi‑Fi and confirm free storage before starting.
downloads.legacyBody: These older all-in-one downloads still work offline. Delete when no longer needed and use the smaller corridor packs above.
downloads.legacyRetiredNote: Retired — still available offline until you delete it. New downloads use smaller corridor packs.
downloads.customBody: Draw a rectangle on the map or use a quick download around your GPS position. Use Wi‑Fi for large areas.
downloads.customAwaitingGps: Open the map tab and wait for GPS to estimate a custom download area.
downloads.customNoGpsBody: A GPS fix is needed to centre the custom download rectangle.
downloads.customSavedBody: Previously downloaded custom squares — delete when no longer needed.
downloads.customMapLead: Pan and zoom the chart, then tap two corners of a square around your sailing area.
downloads.customMapBanner: Tap two corners of a square on the chart
downloads.customStepOne: Step 1 — tap one corner of the square
downloads.customStepTwo: Step 2 — tap the opposite corner (snaps to a square)
downloads.customStepConfirm: Review size and confirm download on Wi‑Fi
downloads.customCornerFirst: First corner set — tap the opposite corner of the square

customInvalid.too_small: Square is too small — zoom in or tap corners farther apart.
customInvalid.too_large: Square is too large — select a smaller area.
customInvalid.too_many_tiles: Too many map tiles — reduce area or lower max zoom.

downloads.customStartedBody: Return to Downloads to watch progress. Stay on Wi‑Fi until complete.
downloads.statusSummaryActiveInitializing: Preparing %{name} — chart engine is loading tiles
downloads.statusSummaryActiveHint: Stay on Wi‑Fi until the download finishes.
downloads.statusSummaryReadyHint: Charts and seamarks work without mobile signal in downloaded areas.
downloads.statusSummaryEmpty: Recently viewed charts are cached automatically but may have gaps. Download a corridor pack below before you lose signal.
downloads.statusSummaryFailedHint: Check your connection, then tap Try again on the pack below.

failureModal.title: Chart download failed
failureModal.reportHint: Copy this report and send it for debugging. It contains no passwords or personal data.

kielBay.name: Kieler Bucht (test)
kielBay.description: Small pack for dockside and on-water UAT around Kiel.

kattegatNorth.name: Kattegat north
kattegatNorth.description: Skagen to Anholt — open Kattegat crossing and north Jutland coast.

kattegatSouth.name: Kattegat south
kattegatSouth.description: East Jutland and Limfjord approaches — passage between North and Baltic seas.

danishBelts.name: Danish belts
danishBelts.description: Great Belt, Little Belt, and Fehmarn Belt — strait detail for belt passages.

oresund.name: Øresund
oresund.description: Copenhagen–Malmö strait and Sound approaches — harbour-scale detail.

germanBalticWest.name: German Baltic west
germanBalticWest.description: Flensburg to Fehmarn and Lübeck Bay.

germanBalticEast.name: German Baltic east
germanBalticEast.description: Wismar to Rügen and Szczecin lagoon approach.

skaneCoast.name: Skåne coast
skaneCoast.description: Malmö to Ystad and southern Skåne sailing grounds.

blekinge.name: Blekinge
blekinge.description: Karlskrona archipelago and eastern Blekinge coast.

kalmarSound.name: Kalmar & Öland west
kalmarSound.description: Kalmar Sound, Öland west coast, and Västervik approaches.

gotlandWest.name: Gotland west
gotlandWest.description: Visby west coast and Gotland passage waters.

gotlandEast.name: Gotland east
gotlandEast.description: Gotland east coast and Hoburg approaches.

gulfFinlandWest.name: Gulf of Finland west
gulfFinlandWest.description: Helsinki archipelago south and Gulf of Finland western waters.

alandBothniaSouth.name: Åland & Bothnian Sea south
alandBothniaSouth.description: Åland islands, Turku archipelago, and southern Bothnian Sea.

balticWest.name: Kattegat & Danish Straits (legacy)
balticWest.description: Former all-in-one pack — replaced by Kattegat, belts, and Øresund corridor packs.

balticSouth.name: German Baltic coast (legacy)
balticSouth.description: Former all-in-one pack — replaced by German Baltic west/east corridor packs.

balticSouthSe.name: Southern Sweden (legacy)
balticSouthSe.description: Former all-in-one pack — replaced by Skåne, Blekinge, and Kalmar corridor packs.

balticCentral.name: Central Baltic (legacy)
balticCentral.description: Former all-in-one pack — replaced by Gotland west/east corridor packs.

balticEast.name: Gulf of Finland approach (legacy)
balticEast.description: Former all-in-one pack — replaced by Gulf of Finland west corridor pack.

balticNorth.name: Gulf of Bothnia south & Åland (legacy)
balticNorth.description: Former all-in-one pack — replaced by Åland & Bothnian Sea south corridor pack.

waypoints.subtitle: Saved marks and harbours
waypoints.emptyBody: Long-press the map to drop a waypoint, or add a sample mark to get started.
waypoints.tapGoTo: Tap a waypoint to go-to · long-press to delete
waypoints.deleteFailed: Could not delete waypoint. Try again.
waypoints.selectHint: Select a waypoint to edit details or go-to.

tracks.subtitle: Record your route and export GPX
tracks.emptyBody: Start recording while underway. Points are saved every few seconds with GPS.
tracks.locationRequiredBody: Allow location access to record a track.
tracks.backgroundNotificationBody: Recording your route — GPS active in background
tracks.recordingBackground: Background GPS active (10 s moving / 60 s at anchor)
tracks.backgroundDeniedBody: Track will record only while SeaCheck is open. Enable “Always” in system settings for passage logging with screen off.
tracks.selectHint: Select a track to view stats, export GPX, or delete.
tracks.pointInspectHint: Tap the track line on the map to inspect a fix

passage.subtitle: Plan legs before departure
passage.emptyBody: Create a passage and add at least two waypoints for leg bearings and ETAs.
passage.deleteFromListHint: Deletes this passage after you confirm
passage.deleteFailed: Could not delete passage. Try again.
passage.listSubtitle: Tap a passage to plan and edit — reverse or delete from the list
passage.nameHint: Change the name here — press Done on the keyboard or tap Save.
passage.selectHint: Select a passage to edit waypoints.
passage.needTwoWaypoints: Add at least two waypoints to this passage.
passage.activateNeedTwo: A passage needs at least two waypoints before it can be activated.
passage.routeSummaryA11y: Planned route: %{legs} legs, %{distance} %{unit}, %{hours} hours at planned speed
passage.waypointsBody: Waypoints are visited in order. Plan on the chart or enter coordinates.
passage.waypointsEmpty: No waypoints yet — tap Plan on map or Add by coordinates.
passage.noMoreWaypoints: All saved waypoints are in this passage — use Plan on map or Add by coordinates for more.
passage.offlineCheckBody: Checks whether downloaded packs cover each leg (with 0.5 NM buffer). Download gaps before departure.
passage.mapPreviewOffline: Map preview requires offline charts — download a pack first
passage.offlineNoPacks: No offline packs ready — download charts first
passage.recommendedPacksBody: These corridor packs cover the gaps below. Download one at a time on Wi‑Fi.
passage.recommendedPacksCustomHint: Some legs still need a custom map area — tap the button below.
passage.remainingLegsAfterPacks: %{count} leg(s) may still need a custom area after these downloads.
passage.downloadAllPartialFail: Some downloads failed — retry individually on Wi‑Fi.
passage.offlineNeedsCustom: This route crosses areas outside corridor packs. Draw a custom download rectangle in Downloads.
passage.reverseHint: Reverses the order of all waypoints in this passage
passage.reverseFromListHint: Reverses waypoint order for this passage
passage.reverseNeedTwo: Add at least two waypoints before reversing the route
passage.reverseFailed: Could not reverse route. Try again.
passage.reverseActiveBody: Reversing while navigating changes your live go-to target and leg alarms. Only proceed if you intend to sail the route in the opposite direction.
passage.nextLegManualHint: Advances to the next passage leg manually
passage.skipToNextWaypointHint: Stop navigating to the current waypoint and continue with the next one — use after tacking or shortcuts
passage.waypointPassedAlongTrack: You passed %{name} — tap Skip to continue
passage.waypointArrivedNear: Near %{name} — tap Skip when ready for the next leg
passage.followBannerA11y: Following %{passage}, leg %{leg} of %{total}. Next waypoint %{name}. Bearing %{brg}, distance %{dist}.
passage.navHeroA11y: Navigate to %{name}. Bearing %{brg} degrees %{suffix}, distance %{dist} %{unit}. Leg %{leg} of %{total}.
passage.planOnMapHint: Open the chart to add waypoints by tapping
passage.showPassageOnMapHint: Open the chart to view this route
passage.manageOnPassageScreenHint: Opens the passage tab to edit waypoints and details
passage.coordsInvalid: Enter valid numbers for latitude and longitude.
passage.mapPassageStartFailed: Could not start passage planning. Try again.
passage.mapPlanningWaypointHint: Change name or coordinates below, or delete this waypoint.
passage.mapPlanningGuideStepLongPress: Press and hold the chart to place a waypoint
passage.mapPlanningGuideStepTapEdit: Tap a waypoint to edit name and position
passage.mapPlanningBannerShort: Hold chart to add waypoint · tap waypoint to edit
passage.mapPlanningViewBannerShort: View only — edit route in Passage tab
passage.mapPlanningBanner: Passage planning — hold chart to add waypoints
passage.mapPlanningViewBanner: Active passage — view only on chart
passage.mapPlanningTapHint: Hold the chart to add waypoints. Tap a waypoint to edit.
passage.mapPlanningViewHint: Viewing your route on the chart. Tap a waypoint for details. Manage or unlock editing in the Passage tab.
passage.mapPlanningActiveEditHint: Hold the chart to add waypoints. Tap a waypoint to edit.
passage.mapPlanningActiveBody: Your underway route opens in view-only mode on the chart. Unlock editing separately if you need to change it.
passage.mapPlanningUnlockBody: Changing waypoints while navigating can alter your live targets and alarms. Only proceed if you intend to update the route.
passage.mapPlanningNeedSecond: Hold the chart once more to add a second waypoint, then you can activate navigation.
passage.mapPlanningEmpty: No waypoints yet — press and hold the chart to place the first one.
passage.mapPlanningReadyHint: Hold the chart to add more waypoints. Tap any waypoint to edit.
passage.mapPlanningPaused: Passage planning paused for chart download
passage.mapPlanningSwitchBody: You are already planning another passage on the chart. Switch to this one?
passage.mapPlanningDiscardEmpty: This passage has no waypoints and will be deleted.
passage.activated: Passage activated — follow on the chart
